population_setting: newly diagnosed moderate-to-severe OSA without concomitant respiratory disease
exposure_definition: percentage of total sleep time spent with SaO2 <90%
outcome_definition: death over 5-year follow-up; all-cause mortality from civil registry
adjustment_summary: unadjusted odds-ratio analysis using chi-square/Fisher comparisons in GraphPad Prism; no multivariable adjustment reported
source_location: Results paragraph and Table 1 in supplied PDF
notes: Full text confirms an unadjusted threshold analysis with 8% mortality for T90 <=20% versus 18% for T90 >20%; no separate extractable nadir-SaO2 mortality estimate was reported.

population_setting: community-based adults with symptom data and high-quality oxygen signals, free of baseline CVD
osa_definition: OSA-framed SHHS cohort; no baseline CVD
exposure_definition: respiration-event-related hypoxic burden, natural log transformed as ln(HB+0.1)
outcome_definition: first major adverse cardiovascular event including CHD, heart failure, stroke, or cardiovascular mortality
adjustment_summary: symptom subtypes plus clinical covariates and additional OSA severity measures including AHI, T90, and oxygen saturation nadir
notes: Incremental model remained null for MACE after adjustment for other OSA severity measures.

population_setting: consecutive adults undergoing coronary artery bypass surgery with preoperative sleep study
osa_definition: sleep-disordered breathing defined preoperatively by AHI >=5 or ODI >=5
exposure_definition: ODI >=5 events/hour defining sleep-disordered breathing with nocturnal hypoxaemia
outcome_definition: atrial fibrillation after coronary artery bypass surgery requiring pharmacological intervention or cardioversion before discharge
adjustment_summary: age, left ventricular function, aortic cross-clamp time, maximum postoperative lactate dehydrogenase, and ODI-defined disordered breathing
notes: Abstract-level extraction only. ODI-defined disordered breathing, not AHI, was the independent postoperative AF predictor in this specialized CABG cohort.

population_setting: community-dwelling Japanese adults initially free of ischemic heart disease and stroke
osa_definition: nocturnal intermittent hypoxia as a surrogate marker of OSA in a community cohort
outcome_definition: incident composite cardiovascular disease including stroke and coronary heart disease
adjustment_summary: age, sex, body mass index, alcohol, and smoking
notes: Community-based nocturnal intermittent hypoxia paper with extractable adjusted CVD-composite HR. Retain as general-population OSA-surrogate sensitivity evidence rather than as a core OSA cohort.

population_setting: adults with moderate-to-severe OSA and coronary artery disease
exposure_definition: high hypoxic burden defined above the cohort median of 60.7 %min/h
exposure_scale: high vs low HB by median split
outcome_definition: incident first major adverse cardiovascular and cerebrovascular event
adjustment_summary: multivariable model in the observational RICCADSA cohort; detailed covariate list not fully recovered from the abstract text
sleep_study_type: sleep study in coronary artery disease cohort
notes: Specialized secondary-prevention cohort. High HB, but not AHI >=30/h, was associated with MACCEs. Keep as a specialized HB-MACCE sensitivity/comparator row rather than merge with the default pooled core.
